content.problemStatement:
The chef is playing a game with a coin that has a 111 printed on one side and a 222 printed on the other. He wants to find out how many different ways he can achieve a sum of SSS by flipping the coin any number of times.

Help Chef determine the total number of ways to get a sum of SSS using any number of flips of the coin.


Input Format
The input consists of a single integer SSS.



Output Format
Output a single integer representing the number of ways to achieve the sum SSS.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer S (1 ≤ S ≤ 20), the target sum.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the number of different ordered sequences of coin flips that sum to S.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For S=1 only (1) → 1 way. For S=2 sequences (1,1) and (2) → 2 ways. For S=3 sequences (1,1,1), (1,2), (2,1) → 3 ways.
dp[20] follows the Fibonacci recurrence and equals 10946.

solution.approach: Each flip contributes either 1 or 2 to the total sum. Let dp[x] be the number of ordered sequences that sum to x. For x=0 there is one empty sequence, so dp[0]=1. For x=1 only (1) is possible, so dp[1]=1. For any x≥2 the last flip can be a 1 (leaving a sum of x‑1) or a 2 (leaving a sum of x‑2). Hence dp[x]=dp[x‑1]+dp[x‑2]. This is exactly the Fibonacci recurrence shifted by one. Pre‑compute dp up to the maximum S (20) once and answer each test case in O(1).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) because the pre‑computation for MAX_S=20 is constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space (dp array of size 21).

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    MAX_S=20
    dp=[0]*(MAX_S+1)
    dp[0]=1
    dp[1]=1
    for i in range(2,MAX_S+1):
        dp[i]=dp[i-1]+dp[i-2]
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        S=int(next(it))
        out.append(str(dp[S]))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
